name: Alex Foster
abstract: Alex Foster (born May 10, 1953) is an American jazz musician who plays alto and tenor saxophone. He has recorded for record labels since the early 1970s.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: We Remember Erroll - A Tribute to Erroll Garner
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Benny Goodman Revisited: 1993
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Blues in Jazz
Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The New Legacy

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Jon Faddis, Alex Foster, Bill Evans the tenor player, myself on the trombone... That was the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: And the first band... had Faddis and Soloff playing trumpets and Jimmy and I were playing trombone, Eddie Daniels was in the band, Alex Foster, Jimmy Madison played drums and Jay Leonhart played bass. It was a really good band that he called the New York All-Star Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: So I hired Alex, and we had a band and we started rehearsing... So the band was called Directions... Well, Mike left and Alex left.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: Jack formed a band with myself and Alex Foster, a saxophone player, and Mike Richmond on bass. And we toured for a while as a band
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Let me just read the personnel list. In addition to you and him, Alex Foster... [INTERVIEWEE] I didn’t know that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
